originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Happy News from Djibouti! President Guelleh Continues to Lead!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! I'm your special news reporter, bringing you exciting stories from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we're going on a little adventure to a beautiful country called Djibouti. Can you say that? Dee-j-boo-tee! It's a small but very important country on the map of Africa, right by the sparkling blue sea. In Djibouti, just like in many countries, people have a special leader called a President. A President is like the head teacher of a whole country. They help make sure everyone is safe, happy, and that the country grows stronger every day. It's a very big and important job!

Meet President Guelleh!

Our news today is about the President of Djibouti. His name is Mr. Ismail Omar Guelleh. He is a wise man, 78 years old. That's older than many of your grandmas and grandpas! He has been the President for a very, very long time already – 27 years! Imagine being a leader for 27 years! That's like being in charge since before most of your parents were even grown-ups. He has seen Djibouti change and grow so much during all those years. He cares a lot about his country and its people. **Did you know?** Djibouti is a country in East Africa. It's famous for its warm weather and beautiful salt lakes!

What is an Election? (Choosing a Leader!)

Sometimes, in a country, people get to choose their leader. This is called an election. It's a special time when grown-ups can pick who they think will do the best job as President. It's like when you choose your favorite game to play, but much bigger! To choose, people vote. A vote is like saying 'I choose this person!' You mark a special paper or press a button to show who you want to be the leader. Every vote is important because it helps decide who will guide the country. **Did you know?** In many countries, you have to be at least 18 years old to vote! That's how grown-up you need to be to make such an important choice.

President Guelleh Stays to Lead!

Recently, Djibouti had an election. President Guelleh wanted to continue being the leader, and so did some other people. When all the votes were counted, guess what? President Guelleh won! Almost everyone who voted chose him. A huge number, 97.8% of the people who voted, said 'Yes!' to him. This means he will continue to be the President of Djibouti for more years. It's like winning a really big game and getting to play again! The people who voted showed that they trust him to keep helping Djibouti. This will be his sixth time being President. We call each time a President is chosen a 'term'. So, this is his sixth term! That's a lot of turns at being the country's leader. He must really enjoy helping his country!

Some People Chose Not to Vote

Now, here's something a little different. In this election, some other people who wanted to be President decided not to take part. These are called 'opposition candidates' – they are people who had different ideas about how to lead the country. They chose not to vote or let people vote for them this time. It's like if you were playing a game, and some of your friends decided they didn't want to play this round. They have their reasons for doing that. But many other people still went to vote, and they chose President Guelleh. **Did you know?** Sometimes, people don't agree on everything, and that's okay! It's important to talk and listen to each other's ideas and be respectful.

More About Amazing Djibouti!

Djibouti is a fantastic place! It's not a very big country, but it has a very important spot on the map. It's like a special gate between land and sea, making it very important for ships and trade. Many ships from all over the world stop there! The country has a beautiful coastline, with clear waters perfect for swimming and seeing amazing sea creatures. It also has incredible landscapes, like the famous Lake Assal, which is one of the saltiest lakes in the world! Imagine a lake saltier than the ocean! People in Djibouti are known for being very friendly and welcoming. They speak different languages, like Arabic and French, and they have rich traditions and delicious food. It's a place full of interesting things to learn and see. **Did you know?** Djibouti is home to a special kind of fish called the whale shark, which is the biggest fish in the world! They are gentle giants of the ocean and eat tiny little creatures.

A Bright Future Ahead!

So, with President Guelleh continuing to lead, the people of Djibouti are looking forward to a bright future. He will keep working hard to make sure Djibouti stays safe, happy, and strong. He will help schools, hospitals, and make sure there are good jobs for everyone. We wish President Guelleh and all the wonderful people of Djibouti the very best as they continue to build their beautiful country. It's always exciting to see our African countries working together to grow and succeed!
summary: President Ismail Omar Guelleh of Djibouti will continue to lead his country after winning a recent election, trusted by many people to keep helping Djibouti grow.
whyMatters: This news is important because the President helps lead the whole country, making big choices about schools, safety, and making sure everyone has a good life. When a leader stays on for a long time, it means many people want them to keep guiding the country's future.
